description_long: This object is a low-profile, heavy-duty hydraulic floor jack, commonly used in automotive garages for lifting vehicles. It features a robust, rectangular steel body painted in a worn red finish, with visible scuffs and signs of use. The jack has four wheels: two larger fixed wheels at the rear and two smaller pivoting wheels at the front, allowing for easy maneuverability. A long, detachable metal handle with a black grip is positioned alongside the jack, used for pumping and operating the lifting mechanism. The lifting pad is circular and sits atop a short, sturdy arm, while the main body displays warning labels and operational instructions. The overall construction is solid, with exposed bolts and reinforced seams, indicating durability and industrial use.
description: A heavy-duty red hydraulic floor jack with four wheels and a detachable metal handle, designed for lifting vehicles in garages. The jack shows signs of wear and features a robust steel body with a circular lifting pad.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, rectangular body, two front wheels, and the circular lifting pad; the handle is placed to the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the low-profile steel body, all four wheels, the lifting arm, and the full length of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the handle socket, rear wheels, and the top surface with visible labels and the lifting pad.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the steel body, wheels, and lifting mechanism from the other side.
attribute_wheel_configuration: four wheels, two large rear and two small front
